Engineered for the rigorous demands of soft-tissue fixation and repair, FiberWire sutures provide an optimal balance of strength, handling, and consistency, supporting both routine procedures and advanced fixation constructs. Renowned for their versatility, they are widely used in orthopedic and sports medicine applications for tendon and ligament repair throughout the body.1 อธ Patented core design for superior strength and handling2 อธ More than 50 million strands sold 3 อธ Most versatile offering of sizes, colors, and needles for use throughout the body อธ Sizes range from 4-0 through #5 อธ Collagen-coated and flat, tape-style suture options also available อธ Industry-leading number of specialty suture options อธ Next-generation SutureTape offers improved handling characteristics, resists tissue pull-through, and creates tighter, smaller knot stacks 4 FiberWireยฎ Sutures | 05 SutureTape At the Forefront of Suture Technology Engineered for strength and precision, flat-braided SutureTape delivers superior resistance to tissue pull-through compared to #2 suture.โด Its innovative design enhances handling and knot security while minimizing knot stack size for a cleaner, more efficient closure. Available in 0.9 mm, 1.3 mm, and 1.7 mm widths, SutureTape comes in a variety of colors, patterns, and needle configurations to meet the demands of any surgical technique. SutureTape provides a wider footprint and broader tissue compression for the repair, while also providing improved handling characteristics. The load at 3 mm of displacement was 39% higher for 1.3 mm SutureTape than the #2 suture.4 SutureTape, X-pattern, (1 blue/black, 1 black/white) 1.3 mm, 102 cm long, w/ tapered cutting needle, 36.6 mm, 1/2 circle (AR-7501T) SutureTape, 1.3 mm, 40 in (black/white), w/ tapered needle, 36.6 mm, ยฝ circle (AR-7506T) SutureTape, 1.3 mm, 40 in (blue/dark blue), w/ tapered needle, 36.6 mm, ยฝ circle (AR-7506) SutureTape, 1.7 mm, 38 in (white/blue) w/ conventional cutting needle, 48 mm, 1/2 circle (AR-7511) Tighter, smaller knot stacks compared to #2 suture 1 Pull-Out Strength With Surgeon Knot 4 Load at 3 mm Displacement (lbf) 80 70 60 50 40 30 20 10 0 SutureTape #2 Suture 64 46 06 | FiberWireยฎ Sutures FiberWireยฎ and TigerWireยฎ Sutures High-Strength Suture Introduced in 2001, FiberWire suture has more than two decades of clinical use and remains a trusted suture for soft -tissue fixation in orthopedic surgery. Engineered with a multistrand core of ultra-high molecular weight polyethylene (UHMWPE) and encased in a braided jacket of polyester and UHMWPE, FiberWire suture offers exceptional durability and performance. It is available in sizes ranging from 4-0 to #5, with a variety of colors and needle configurations to suit diverse surgical needs. TigerWire suture builds on this same high-strength design, incorporating a distinctive black marker strand to enhance visibility and simplify identification during joint reconstruction and soft-tissue repair procedures. Keep your sutures organized with the FiberWire cart, which holds up to 96 boxes of suture. (AR-7200CART) #5 FiberWire Suture, 38 in (blue) w/ conventional cutting needle, 48 mm, ยฝ circle (AR-7211) #2 FiberWire Suture, 38 in (1 blue, 1 white/ black) w/ tapered needle, 26.5 mm, ยฝ circle (AR-7208) #2 FiberWire Suture, 38 in (blue) w/ tapered needle, 26.5 mm, 1/2 circle (AR-7200) #2 FiberWire Suture, 38 in (1 white, 1 blue/black) (AR-7240) FiberWireยฎ Sutures | 07 FiberTapeยฎ Suture Leading Suture for Knotless Repairs FiberTape suture is the leading choice for knotless repairs, offering broad compression and superior resistance to tissue pull-through. This ultra-high- strength suture features a long-chain polyethylene structure, similar to FiberWire ยฎ suture, ensuring durability and reliability in demanding repairs. The 2 mm FiberTape suture includes a #2 core, making it ideal for repairs involving degenerative cuff tissue where tissue pull-through is a concern. Its wide footprint enhances load distribution and is 27% more resistant to tissue pull-through compared to traditional round #2 sutures.โต For procedures requiring a smaller profile, 1.7 mm FiberTape suture is also available, featuring a size 0 core. This option delivers the same high-strength performance in a more compact design, giving surgeons flexibility based on tissue quality and repair technique 1.7 mm FiberTape used in a FiberTak ยฎ SpeedBridgeโข repair Brostrom repair with Internal Braceโข ligament augmentation using FiberTape suture Easily manage sutures with unique instruments designed for tape-style suture TigerTape Suture, 1.7 mm, 8 in working length, 30 in overall (white/black), each end
